title: impatiens digitata subsp. digitata
intro: Impatiens digitata subsp. digitata, also known as the common or swamp jewelweed, is a subspecies of plant belonging to the balsam family that is native to North America. The medicinal properties of this plant lie in its high concentration of hypericin and hyperforin, compounds that have been shown to exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Impatiens digitata subsp. digitata include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and insect bites, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antibacterial agent to aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into salves, ointments, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
